proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 33 anonymized features, several continuous and binary variables are associated with pfs_months in pairwise (univariate) tests. Specifically, feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90) is positively correlated with pfs_months, and feature_023 (binary) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; import pandas as pd; df=pd.read_parquet('dataset.parquet'); stats.pearsonr(df['feature_015'], df['pfs_months']); stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_023']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_023']==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Univariate screen of all features against pfs_months. feature_015 has Pearson r=+0.757 with pfs_months (p<1e-300). feature_023=1 vs 0: mean pfs 2.925 vs 4.362, diff=-1.44 months (t-test p<1e-300). Both highly significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Other binary features show non-trivial main effects on pfs_months: feature_011=1 lowers pfs (negative), feature_016=1 raises pfs (positive), feature_028=1 lowers pfs, feature_018=1 raises pfs, and squamous histology (feature_006=='squamous') lowers pfs vs adenocarcinoma.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: t,p = stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df[c]==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df[c]==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Univariate t-tests: feature_011=1 mean pfs 2.74 vs 3.66 (diff=-0.92, p<1e-300); feature_016=1 mean pfs 4.11 vs 3.33 (diff=+0.78, p=4.8e-151); feature_028=1 mean pfs 2.66 vs 3.47 (diff=-0.81, p=2.9e-69); feature_018=1 mean pfs 3.64 vs 3.32 (diff=+0.32, p=1.6e-53); squamous=1 mean pfs 2.81 vs adenocarcinoma 3.67 (diff=-0.86, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014, an ordinal 3-level variable (0/1/2), shows a monotone negative association with pfs_months, with each higher level corresponding to lower mean pfs.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_014')['pfs_months'].mean(); ols('pfs_months ~ feature_014', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by feature_014: 0->4.327 (n=17557), 1->3.185 (n=24962), 2->2.152 (n=7481). Linear fit coef=-1.099 per unit, p<1e-300; ANOVA F=3141, p<1e-300. Strongly monotonic decrease.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (smoking status: never/former/current) is associated with pfs_months: current smokers have lower pfs than former or never smokers.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_001')['pfs_months'].mean(); stats.f_oneway(*[df.loc[df['feature_001']==v,'pfs_months'] for v in df['feature_001'].unique()])
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: never=3.675 (n=7459), former=3.607 (n=27567), current=2.987 (n=14974). One-way ANOVA F=433, p=3.7e-187. Current smokers ~0.6-0.7 months lower than never/former.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for all features, the strongest independent prognostic predictors of pfs_months are feature_015 (positive), feature_014 (negative), feature_023 (negative), feature_011 (negative), feature_016 (positive), feature_018 (positive), squamous histology (negative), and feature_017 (negative).

analyses.code: ols('pfs_months ~ ' + ' + '.join(all_features), data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Full multivariable OLS with all 33 features (R^2=0.894). Significant coefficients (per-unit, signed): feature_015 +0.168 (p<1e-300); feature_014 -1.10 (p<1e-300); feature_023 -1.42 (p<1e-300); feature_011 -0.91 (p<1e-300); feature_016 +0.89 (p<1e-300); feature_018 +0.32 (p<1e-300); squamous -0.72 (p<1e-300); feature_017 -0.073 per unit (p<1e-300); feature_025 +0.46 (p<1e-300); feature_031 -0.20 (p<1e-300); feature_001 (former/never vs current) +0.55 (p<1e-300). Other features (e.g., 022, 002, 005, 028) have small or null adjusted effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_015 and pfs_months is positive overall, but exhibits curvature (a quadratic component) so higher levels of feature_015 confer disproportionately greater pfs.

analyses.code: ols('pfs_months ~ feature_015 + I(feature_015**2)', df).fit(); df.assign(q=pd.qcut(df['feature_015'],5,labels=False)).groupby('q')['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Quintile mean pfs across feature_015 (range,n,mean): Q1[30-56.5,1.14], Q2[56.6-62.4,2.42], Q3[62.5-67.6,3.37], Q4[67.7-73.5,4.36], Q5[73.6-90,5.92]. Quadratic regression: linear coef +0.016 (p=0.011), quadratic coef +0.00119 (p=3e-136). Strong positive curvature confirms accelerating relationship.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_017 (continuous, range 0-28) has a small negative association with pfs_months, with patients in the highest quartile having shorter pfs than those in the lowest quartile.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df['feature_017'], df['pfs_months']); df.assign(q=pd.qcut(df['feature_017'],4,labels=False,duplicates='drop')).groupby('q')['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=-0.127, p=7.5e-179. Quartile means: Q0[0-1.7]=3.70, Q1[1.8-4.3]=3.46, Q2[4.4-7.2]=3.30, Q3[7.3-28]=2.99. Monotone decrease of ~0.7 months across quartiles.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_025 (continuous, range 1.7-5.5, mean 3.8) has a positive association with pfs_months: higher feature_025 corresponds to higher pfs.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df['feature_025'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.099, p=2.8e-109. After adjusting in multivariable model, coef=+0.46 per unit (p<1e-300). Modest but highly significant positive prognostic effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Several binary features are likely candidate randomized/treatment-like assignments because their prevalence is balanced across feature_014 stage and feature_023 status: feature_012 (~50%), feature_018 (~35%), feature_020 (~30%), feature_027 (~30%), feature_021 (~28%). These are candidate 'treatment' variables for heterogeneity analysis.

analyses.code: for f in candidate_treatments: print(f, df.groupby('feature_014')[f].mean(), df.groupby('feature_023')[f].mean())
analyses.result_summary: feature_012 prevalence by stage 0/1/2: 0.496/0.501/0.508; by feature_023 0/1: 0.499/0.501 - balanced. feature_018: 0.354/0.352/0.342 by stage; 0.350/0.351 by feature_023 - balanced. feature_020: 0.295/0.303/0.302 - balanced. feature_027: 0.299/0.300/0.300 - balanced. feature_021: 0.286/0.284/0.286 - balanced. All five look like randomly assigned treatment-like variables.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the candidate treatments, feature_018 has a positive main effect on pfs_months (+0.32 months), feature_021 has a small negative main effect (-0.19 months), while feature_012, feature_020, and feature_027 have no significant main effect.

analyses.code: for tx in candidates: t,p = stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df[tx]==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df[tx]==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Main effect t-tests: feature_018 +0.322 (p=1.6e-53); feature_021 -0.190 (p=9.1e-18); feature_012 -0.035 (p=0.08); feature_020 -0.025 (p=0.25); feature_027 +0.004 (p=0.87). feature_018 is a beneficial treatment-like variable; feature_021 mildly harmful; others null on average.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_018 treatment effect on pfs_months is heterogeneous and concentrated in patients with feature_016=1, with negligible benefit in patients with feature_016=0 (treatment-by-biomarker interaction).

analyses.code: ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018*feature_016', df).fit(); for v in [0,1]: sub=df[df['feature_016']==v]; sub.loc[sub['feature_018']==1,'pfs_months'].mean()-sub.loc[sub['feature_018']==0,'pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: feature_018 effect within feature_016=0 (n=43,632): -0.005 months (n.s.). feature_018 effect within feature_016=1 (n=6,368): +2.552 months. Interaction coefficient feature_018:feature_016 = +2.558 (t=41.9, p<1e-300). The treatment effect is essentially zero in feature_016=0 and very large in feature_016=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_018 benefit, even within the feature_016=1 biomarker-positive subgroup, is further restricted to patients with feature_031=0 — patients with feature_031=1 derive no benefit despite feature_016=1.

analyses.code: ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018*feature_016*feature_031', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: 3-way interaction model: feature_018:feature_016 = +4.66 (p<1e-300); feature_018:feature_016:feature_031 = -4.69 (p<1e-300). Empirical subgroup tx effects: feature_016=1,feature_031=0 (n=3508): +4.64 months; feature_016=1,feature_031=1 (n=2860): -0.01 months; feature_016=0,either feature_031: ~0. The benefit is fully extinguished by feature_031=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_018 'responder' subgroup (feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0), the benefit is further suppressed by feature_028=1 and by feature_005=1 — both small subgroups where feature_018 yields no benefit (or slight harm).

analyses.code: for f in ['feature_028','feature_005']: for v in [0,1]: sub=df[(df['feature_016']==1)&(df['feature_031']==0)&(df[f]==v)]; e=sub.loc[sub['feature_018']==1,'pfs_months'].mean()-sub.loc[sub['feature_018']==0,'pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0: feature_028=0 (n=3370) tx_eff=+4.83; feature_028=1 (n=138) tx_eff=-0.29. feature_005=0 (n=3401) tx_eff=+4.80; feature_005=1 (n=107) tx_eff=-0.07. Both feature_028=1 and feature_005=1 fully abolish the benefit in this otherwise responder cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete 'feature_018 responder' subgroup is defined by all four predicates: feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0. Within this n~3266 subgroup the treatment effect is approximately +5 pfs_months; outside it the effect is null.

analyses.code: df['responder']=((df['feature_016']==1)&(df['feature_031']==0)&(df['feature_028']==0)&(df['feature_005']==0)).astype(int); ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018*responder', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Responder cohort n=3266 (6.5% of dataset). Within: feature_018=1 mean pfs 7.876 vs feature_018=0 mean pfs 3.232; tx_eff=+4.99 months (p<1e-300). Median pfs 8.21 vs 3.23. Outside responder: tx_eff=-0.006 months (p=0.76, null). Interaction term feature_018:responder = +4.99 (t=62.5, p<1e-300). This 4-predicate subgroup fully captures the treatment-effect heterogeneity for feature_018.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 treatment effect on pfs_months does NOT vary meaningfully by feature_014 (stage) — the benefit is similar across all three stage levels, indicating biomarker (feature_016) — not stage — drives heterogeneity.

analyses.code: ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018*C(feature_014)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_018 effect by feature_014: stage 0=+0.313 (p=1.4e-19), stage 1=+0.314 (p=1.3e-29), stage 2=+0.296 (p=2.0e-10). Interaction p-values >0.5 for both stage 1 and stage 2 vs stage 0. Stage does not modify the feature_018 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 treatment effect varies by smoking status (feature_001) overall, with current and former smokers showing larger benefit than never smokers; however this is largely explained by feature_016 prevalence differing across smoking groups.

analyses.code: for s in ['never','former','current']: sub=df[df['feature_001']==s]; e=sub.loc[sub['feature_018']==1,'pfs_months'].mean()-sub.loc[sub['feature_018']==0,'pfs_months'].mean(); within feature_016=1: same
analyses.result_summary: Marginal feature_018 effect by smoke: never=+0.014; former=+0.300; current=+0.512. But within feature_016=1: never=+1.76 (n=150), former=+2.66, current=+2.48 — i.e., never smokers within feature_016=1 are nearly identical to other smokers, just rare (n=150). Apparent smoking modification is driven by feature_016=1 prevalence: 0.024 in never, 0.119 in former, 0.197 in current.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 treatment effect is somewhat larger in squamous histology (feature_006='squamous') than in adenocarcinoma; but again this likely reflects differential feature_016 prevalence.

analyses.code: ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018*feat006_squamous', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_018 effect by histology: adenocarcinoma=+0.277, squamous=+0.449. Interaction coef=+0.171, p=1.9e-4. Within feature_016=1 stratum, the histology effect on feature_018 benefit is small (+4.665 in adeno vs +4.642 in squamous), so the marginal modification reflects the higher feature_016 prevalence among squamous patients (0.337) vs adeno (0.121).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_012, feature_020, and feature_027 — the other balanced 'treatment-like' candidates — have no clinically meaningful main effect or treatment heterogeneity on pfs_months; they appear to be inert with respect to outcome.

analyses.code: for tx in ['feature_012','feature_020','feature_027']: ols('pfs_months ~ tx*every_modifier', df).fit() interaction screen
analyses.result_summary: feature_012 main effect -0.035 (p=0.08). Largest interaction (feature_012:feature_017 cont) p=3.2e-4 with coef +0.018 — small. feature_020 main effect -0.025 (p=0.25); no interactions reach p<0.005. feature_027 main effect +0.004 (p=0.87); no interactions reach p<0.005. These three are effectively null treatments in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_021 has a small adverse effect on pfs_months overall (-0.19 months); the effect is essentially absent in the small feature_005=1 subgroup, but is otherwise consistent across binary modifiers.

analyses.code: for v in [0,1]: sub=df[df['feature_005']==v]; e=sub.loc[sub['feature_021']==1,'pfs_months'].mean()-sub.loc[sub['feature_021']==0,'pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: feature_021 effect: feature_005=0 (n=48,481) eff=-0.196 (p=2.7e-18); feature_005=1 (n=1,519) eff=+0.014 (p=0.91). feature_028=1 (n=2,464) eff=-0.406 (p=1e-4) — slightly stronger harm. Largest joint amplifier: feature_028=1 within remaining cohort. Overall feature_021 has a modest, fairly homogeneous adverse effect except in feature_005=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pairwise interactions between candidate treatments (feature_012, _018, _020, _021, _027) are not significant — the treatments do not act multiplicatively when co-administered/co-occurring.

analyses.code: for t1,t2 in combinations(candidates,2): ols(f'pfs_months ~ {t1}*{t2}', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All ten pairwise tx-tx interactions yield p>0.19 (smallest: feature_018:feature_021 p=0.20). No evidence of synergistic or antagonistic effects between the candidate treatments at the marginal level.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 (~65% prevalence) is the strongest single binary prognostic factor and remains highly significant after adjusting for feature_015, feature_014, histology, feature_011, and feature_018-by-feature_016 interaction.

analyses.code: ols('pfs_months ~ feature_023 + feature_015 + feature_014 + feat006_squamous + feature_011 + feature_018*feature_016*feature_031', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_023 adjusted coef = -1.412 (p<1e-300). Effect size barely changes from unadjusted (-1.44) — feature_023 is largely independent of the other predictors and acts as a strong, additive prognostic marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (~25% prevalence) has a substantial negative main effect (-0.91 months) that is consistent across binary modifier strata — i.e., it is a pure prognostic, not an effect-modifier or a treatment.

analyses.code: for mod: for v: sub=df[df[mod]==v]; eff = mean diff of feature_011
analyses.result_summary: feature_011 effect across strata: feature_018=0 -0.918, feature_018=1 -0.926; feature_023 strata: -0.91/-0.92; feature_016 strata: -0.92/-0.89; squamous -0.81 vs adeno -0.96; stage 0/1/2: -1.01/-0.91/-0.83. Effect is highly consistent (range -0.81 to -1.01); no meaningful interaction. A reliable negative prognostic factor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among biomarker x biomarker interactions, feature_016 main effect on pfs_months is suppressed by feature_028 (interaction coef -1.21) and by feature_005 (-0.82); these mirror the suppression pattern observed for feature_018 treatment benefit.

analyses.code: ols('pfs_months ~ feature_016*feature_028', df).fit(); ols('pfs_months ~ feature_016*feature_005', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_016:feature_028 = -1.21 (p=3.5e-16); feature_016:feature_005 = -0.82 (p=2.8e-6). Because feature_016's apparent main effect on pfs is mostly mediated by the feature_018 treatment-effect heterogeneity, these biomarker-biomarker interactions reflect the same suppression: feature_028=1 or feature_005=1 abrogate the benefit feature_018 confers in feature_016=1 patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for all main effects, the feature_018 x feature_016 x feature_031 three-way interaction explains substantial additional variance in pfs_months (R^2 increases markedly), confirming the subgroup-specific treatment effect is not an artifact of confounding.

analyses.code: ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018*feature_016*feature_031 + (all other predictors as main effects)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable model with the 3-way interaction reaches R^2=0.971 (vs 0.894 without). The 3-way term coef remains -4.58 (p<1e-300) and the 2-way feature_018:feature_016 coef remains +4.59 (p<1e-300) after adjustment. Treatment-effect heterogeneity is robust to confounding.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for outcome pfs_months: feature_018 increases progression-free survival by approximately +5 months, but only within the subgroup defined by feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0 (~6.5% of cohort, n=3266). Outside this 4-predicate subgroup the treatment effect is null. The unfavorable values feature_016=0, feature_031=1, feature_028=1, and feature_005=1 each fully suppress the treatment benefit.

analyses.code: df['responder'] = ((df['feature_016']==1)&(df['feature_031']==0)&(df['feature_028']==0)&(df['feature_005']==0)).astype(int); ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018*responder', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Final test of the 4-predicate responder subgroup (n=3266). Within: feature_018 increases mean pfs from 3.232 to 7.876 months (Δ=+4.99 months), median 3.23 -> 8.21 months, t-test p<1e-300. Outside: tx effect = -0.006 (n.s.). Interaction feature_018:responder coef +4.992 (t=62.5, p<1e-300). This is the best-supported, complete treatment-effect heterogeneity hypothesis for pfs_months in this cohort.
